questions.prompt: Grammar — Present perfect: "Be careful — he ___ broken his arm, so he can't play today."
questions.explanation: A past action with a result now (he can't play) takes the present perfect, and with he the auxiliary is has + participle: he has broken his arm. "have" is for I/you/we/they, "is" doesn't form the present perfect, and "had" is the past perfect.

questions.prompt: Vocabulary — Which word is the odd one out?
questions.explanation: Thunder, lightning and fog are all weather words, but a ceiling is part of a room, so it is the odd one out. Sort the set by meaning: three belong to the weather, while ceiling belongs to a building.

questions.prompt: Spelling — Choose the correct -ing form: "She was ___ for clothes when I saw her in town."
questions.explanation: shop has one short vowel + one consonant, so the p doubles before -ing: shopping. "shoping" forgets to double the p, "shoppping" adds one too many, and "shopeing" wrongly inserts an e.

questions.prompt:
Reading — Read: "Nadia moved to Tunis in 2019 and still lives there. She has worked at the same school since she arrived."
Where does Nadia live now?
questions.explanation: The text says she moved to Tunis and "still lives there", so she lives in Tunis now (a). Read for the detail: Sfax and Paris are never mentioned, and "still lives there" tells us she has not left (d is wrong).

questions.prompt: Grammar — Advice: "You've had a headache all day. You ___ rest and drink some water."
questions.explanation: This is friendly advice, so we use should + base verb: You should rest. "should to" is wrong (no to after should), "mustn't" would forbid resting, and "don't have to" only says it's optional rather than recommended.
